description_long: This house features a sprawling, multi-sectioned layout with a combination of hip and gable roofs in a dark color, giving it a classic suburban appearance. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely finished in painted stucco or siding, and the structure is surrounded by neatly arranged trees and landscaped greenery. Multiple window groupings are visible on all sides, with a mix of symmetrical and asymmetrical placements, providing ample natural light. The main entryway is accentuated by a small porch area, flanked by columns and a pathway leading up to the door, with additional landscaping in front. The roofline is complex, with several intersecting planes and a central flat section, possibly for a skylight or rooftop access. The overall design suggests a modern take on the traditional American suburban house, with attention to both curb appeal and functional outdoor space.
description: A large, light-colored suburban house with a complex dark roof, multiple window groupings, and landscaped surroundings. The design features a prominent entryway, intersecting rooflines, and a blend of traditional and modern elements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long facade, multiple windows, and a row of trees along the left side.
description_view_1: Front corner view highlighting the main entryway, porch, and landscaping with trees framing the entrance.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a clear look at the extended roofline and a row of trees along the right side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back garden area, additional windows, and a more private landscaped section.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with small porch, columns, steps, and landscaped approach
